result:
Now I have enough to write a complete, sourced inventory. Let me compile it.

---

## oCMS Cache Layer Inventory

*Primary sources: `../llm-wiki-go/wiki/topics/caching.md`, `../llm-wiki-go/wiki/topics/configuration.md`, `../llm-wiki-go/wiki/topics/login-security.md`, `../llm-wiki-go/wiki/topics/rest-api.md`. Source-code fallbacks listed in ¬ßWiki gaps.*

---

### Layer 1 ‚Äî Config Cache

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/config.go` ‚Äî `ConfigCache` struct |
| **Backend** | In-process Go map (`sync.RWMutex`); internal `SimpleCache` for stats |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None specific; `OCMS_CACHE_TTL` / `OCMS_CACHE_MAX_SIZE` do **not** apply here (see note below) |
| **TTL** | Hardcoded 1 h (`New(time.Hour)`), but the cache is **write-invalidated** ‚Äî TTL is a backstop only |
| **Invalidation triggers** | `InvalidateConfig()` called on any site config update; `InvalidateThemeSettings()` is an alias that also calls this; admin routes bypass the cache entirely (`middleware.LoadSiteConfig(db, nil)`) |
| **Consumers** | `internal/handler/frontend.go` ‚Äî site name, URL, description, logo, custom CSS, OG image, robots settings, theme settings, MCP version, `/.well-known/security.txt` fields; `internal/seo/` ‚Äî sitemap URL; any frontend middleware call to `middleware.LoadSiteConfig(db, cacheManager)` |

---

### Layer 2 ‚Äî Sitemap Cache

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/sitemap.go` ‚Äî `SitemapCache` struct |
| **Backend** | In-process `[]byte` field |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None ‚Äî TTL is hardcoded |
| **TTL** | Hardcoded 1 h (passed as `time.Hour` from `manager.go:68`); re-generated on expiry or invalidation |
| **Invalidation triggers** | `InvalidateSitemap()` on page create/update/delete; `InvalidatePage()` also calls this; `InvalidateContent()` calls this; sitemap is keyed by `site_url` config value (since 0.18.1) ‚Äî changing `site_url` implicitly misses cache |
| **Consumers** | `internal/handler/frontend.go` ‚Äî `GET /sitemap.xml` handler calls `cacheManager.GetSitemap()` |

---

### Layer 3 ‚Äî Page Cache

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/pages.go` ‚Äî `PageCache` struct |
| **Backend** | Two in-process maps: `bySlug` and `byID`, keyed by context-aware string |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None ‚Äî TTL is hardcoded |
| **TTL** | Hardcoded 1 h; primarily invalidation-driven |
| **Cache key shape** | `{language}:{role}:{slug}` and `{language}:{role}:{id}`. Role levels: `anonymous`=0, `public`=0, `editor`=1, `admin`=2 |
| **Invalidation triggers** | `InvalidateContent()` (bulk), `InvalidatePage(id)` (single page + all its language/role variants), `InvalidatePages()` (entire cache); publish/unpublish also triggers invalidation (fixed in 0.18.1) |
| **Consumers** | `internal/handler/frontend.go` ‚Äî public page rendering (slug and ID lookups); `internal/api/v2/pages/service.go` ‚Äî API page reads |

---

### Layer 4 ‚Äî Menu Cache

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/menus.go` ‚Äî `MenuCache` struct; wired via `internal/service/menu.go` |
| **Backend** | In-process map `slug ‚Üí *MenuWithItems` |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None |
| **TTL** | Hardcoded 1 h |
| **Invalidation triggers** | `InvalidateMenus()` on menu or menu-item create/update/delete; `MenuService.InvalidateCache(slug)` can target a single slug |
| **Consumers** | `internal/service/menu.go` ‚Äî `GetMenu()` / `GetMenuForLanguage()`; `internal/handler/frontend.go` ‚Äî theme template menu rendering |

---

### Layer 5 ‚Äî Language Cache

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/languages.go` ‚Äî `LanguageCache` struct |
| **Backend** | In-process slices + `code‚ÜíLanguage` map |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None |
| **TTL** | Hardcoded 1 h |
| **Invalidation triggers** | `InvalidateLanguages()` on language create/update/delete |
| **Consumers** | `internal/middleware/auth.go` and frontend middleware for language resolution; `internal/render/render.go` ‚Äî template rendering needs active language list; `internal/handler/frontend.go` ‚Äî hreflang generation, language detection |

---

### Layer 6 ‚Äî Translation Cache

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/translations.go` ‚Äî `TranslationCache` struct |
| **Backend** | In-process nested map keyed by `{entityType}:{entityID}` |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None |
| **TTL** | Hardcoded 1 h |
| **Invalidation triggers** | `InvalidateTranslations()` (all); `InvalidateTranslation(entityType, entityID)` (single entity) |
| **Consumers** | `internal/handler/frontend.go` ‚Äî per-entity translation lookups during page rendering; multi-language content resolution |

---

### Layer 7 ‚Äî General / SimpleCache

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/manager.go` ‚Äî `Manager.General` field, type `*SimpleCache` |
| **Backend** | In-process TTL map |
| **Env vars / defaults** | None |
| **TTL** | Hardcoded **5 minutes** (`New(5 * time.Minute)`) |
| **Invalidation triggers** | `ClearAll()` calls `m.General.Clear()`; TTL-based automatic eviction every 1 minute |
| **Consumers** | Reserved slot ‚Äî no call sites outside `internal/cache/` currently use it. Its stats are included in `TotalStats()` and cleared by `ClearAll()` |

---

### Layer 8 ‚Äî Distributed Cache (Redis or memory fallback)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Package / file** | `internal/cache/redis.go` / `internal/cache/memory.go` ‚Äî selected by factory; held as `Manager.Distributed` |
| **Backend** | Redis when `OCMS_REDIS_URL` is set; otherwise in-process `MemoryCache` (eviction-bounded). Falls back gracefully to memory on Redis connection failure |
| **Env vars / defaults** | `OCMS_REDIS_URL` (none ‚Üí memory-only), `OCMS_CACHE_PREFIX` (`ocms:`), `OCMS_CACHE_TTL` (`3600` s), `OCMS_CACHE_MAX_SIZE` (`10000` entries) |
| **TTL** | `OCMS_CACHE_TTL` ‚Äî default 3600 s. This is the **only** cache layer where this env var applies |
| **Invalidation triggers** | `ClearAll()` calls `m.Distributed.Clear()`; no per-key invalidation methods are wired through the Manager for this layer |
| **Consumers** | Not yet wired to any domain reads ‚Äî the `Distributed` field is accessible for future use and is health-checked via `/health` |

---

### Layer 9 ‚Äî API Rate Limiter (in-memory, per API key)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Package / file** | `internal/middleware/api.go` ‚Äî `limiterCache[K]` generic type |
| **Backend** | In-process map of `token-bucket ‚Üí limiterEntry`, keyed by API key ID |
| **Env vars / defaults** | `OCMS_API_RATE_LIMIT` (default `100` ‚Äî requests per minute per key) |
| **TTL / eviction** | Idle entries evicted after `limiterCacheTTL = 10 min`; cleanup runs every `limiterCacheCleanupInterval = 5 min`; hard cap of `limiterCacheMaxEntries = 10,000` to bound memory |
| **Invalidation triggers** | Automatic TTL eviction. Cleared on process restart |
| **Consumers** | `middleware.APIRateLimit` ‚Äî applied to all `/api/v2/*` routes |

---

### Layer 10 ‚Äî Login Rate Limiter + Account Lockout (in-memory)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Package / file** | `internal/middleware/` ‚Äî `middleware.NewLoginProtection(LoginProtectionConfig{‚Ä¶})` |
| **Backend** | In-process: token bucket per source IP for rate limiting; per-email failed-attempt counter and lockout state |
| **Env vars / defaults** | **None** ‚Äî all parameters are code constants in `cmd/ocms/main.go`: burst=5, rate=0.5 tok/s, max attempts=5, lockout=15 min, attempt window=15 min |
| **TTL / eviction** | Lockout state cleared on process restart; doubling back-off up to 24 h cap |
| **Invalidation triggers** | Successful login clears the failed-attempt counter for that email; restart wipes all state |
| **Consumers** | `POST /admin/login` handler only |

---

### Session Store (not a cache ‚Äî listed for completeness)

The SCS session store (`internal/session/`) is backed by SQLite, not in-memory. It is not part of the `internal/cache/` system and is not invalidated by `ClearAll()`.

---

## Contradictions (verbatim from wiki)

From `../llm-wiki-go/wiki/topics/configuration.md` ‚Äî **Variable catalog completeness**:

> Three sources catalog `OCMS_*` variables with different scope:
> - [sources/readme.md](../sources/readme.md) ‚Äî the full reference, ~55+ variables ‚Ä¶ Omits `OCMS_API_RATE_LIMIT`.
> - [sources/wiki-configuration.md](../sources/wiki-configuration.md) ‚Äî ~40 variables ‚Ä¶ Includes `OCMS_API_RATE_LIMIT` (default `100`). Omits `OCMS_HCAPTCHA_DISABLED` and `OCMS_REQUIRE_EMBED_PROXY_TOKEN`.
> - [sources/claude.md](../sources/claude.md) ‚Äî working subset of ~25 variables. Includes `OCMS_API_RATE_LIMIT` (default `100`). Omits most `OCMS_REQUIRE_*` gates.
>
> `OCMS_API_RATE_LIMIT` appears in two of three sources but not in the README ‚Äî that is drift rather than a semantic conflict, and this wiki page documents it under *Security and API* above.

No contradictions were found in the caching-specific pages (`topics/caching.md`, `sources/docs-caching.md`, `sources/wiki-caching.md`). The two source pages agree on all facts.

---

## Wiki gaps

Facts that required source-code fallback (not available in the wiki):

| Gap | Source location |
|-----|----------------|
| TTLs for individual domain caches are hardcoded `1h` per cache, not governed by `OCMS_CACHE_TTL` | `internal/cache/config.go:30`, `pages.go:33`, `menus.go:34`, `languages.go:29`, `sitemap.go:31` |
| `OCMS_CACHE_TTL` and `OCMS_CACHE_MAX_SIZE` apply **only** to the `Distributed` cache backend, not the domain caches | `cmd/ocms/main.go:738-744` |
| A 7th cache (`Manager.General`, 5-min TTL) exists but has no active consumers | `internal/cache/manager.go:72` |
| `Manager.Distributed` (Layer 8) is not yet wired to any domain-cache reads ‚Äî it's a reserved slot for future distributed coherence | `internal/cache/manager.go:44-60`, grep confirmed no external `m.Distributed` reads |
| API rate limiter is its own `limiterCache[K]` with 10-min idle eviction and 10,000-entry hard cap | `internal/middleware/api.go:923-932` |
| Login rate limiter / lockout state are entirely in-process with no env-var configuration | `internal/middleware/api.go` + login-security topic (which was from wiki, confirming no env vars) |
